vocabulary.definition: A person who creates content on the video-sharing platform YouTube.
A flat, usually rectangular or square paper container for a letter or document.
A large public green area in a town, used for recreation.
Informal or relaxed in style or manner.
An activity done regularly in one's leisure time for pleasure.
A player in baseball who occupies a base.

corrections.stt_output: the stamp stuck to the wet um
corrections.intended: The stamp stuck to the wet envelope.
corrections.context_hint: The STT misinterprets 'envelope' as 'um' due to phonetic similarity and potential background noise.

corrections.stt_output: the crooked mayors failed to fool them out
corrections.intended: The crooked maze failed to fool the mouse.
corrections.context_hint: The STT confuses 'maze' with 'mayors' and 'mouse' with 'them out' due to similar sounds.

corrections.stt_output: the bit of the tear up whole over for a new white dress
corrections.intended: The baby threw up all over her new white dress.
corrections.context_hint: The STT mishears 'baby' as 'bit' and 'threw up' as 'tear up' due to rapid speech and phonetic overlap.

corrections.stt_output: not much to do in south carolina other than a geese
corrections.intended: There is not much to do in South Carolina other than sit on a beach.
corrections.context_hint: The STT misinterprets the phrase due to unclear articulation and background noise.

corrections.stt_output: the pock has a soccer field
corrections.intended: The park has a soccer field.
corrections.context_hint: The STT mishears 'park' as 'pock' due to phonetic similarity.

corrections.stt_output: the first basement caught the fly ball
corrections.intended: The first baseman caught the fly ball.
corrections.context_hint: Mishearing of 'baseman' as 'basement' due to similar sounds.

corrections.stt_output: he decided to count all the sand on the beach as a hop
corrections.intended: He decided to count all the sand on the beach as a hobby.
corrections.context_hint: Phonetic confusion between 'hop' and 'hobby' likely due to speech rhythm.

corrections.stt_output: she had to taste a butter so much that it made her gap
corrections.intended: She hated the taste of butter so much that it made her gag.
corrections.context_hint: Misinterpretation of 'gag' as 'gap' due to similar phonetic endings.

corrections.stt_output: the wake behind the boat told of the past while the open sea foretold life in the unknown
corrections.intended: The wake behind the boat told of the past while the open sea for told life in the unknown.
corrections.context_hint: Misinterpretation of 'foretold' as 'for told' due to speech pacing.

corrections.stt_output: the kids were smiling from ear to ear
corrections.intended: The kids were smiling from ear to ear.
corrections.context_hint: This output is correct, indicating that the STT performs well in this instance.

corrections.stt_output: quick out there they can we could stop racism by pointing out that they made
corrections.intended: People are really out there thinking we can stop fascism by pointing out that they made
corrections.context_hint: The STT mishears 'people' as 'quick out there' due to rapid speech and phonetic confusion.

corrections.intended: He didn't mean to smash the window.
corrections.context_hint: Phonetic confusion likely due to unclear articulation or rapid speech.

corrections.stt_output: it amounts to finish the nail
corrections.intended: It took him a month to finish the meal.
corrections.context_hint: Misinterpretation of similar-sounding phrases, possibly due to speech speed.
Phonetic similarity between 'sip' and 'city' may have caused confusion.

corrections.stt_output: a five t helps to pass the evening
corrections.intended: A pot of tea helps to pass the evening.
corrections.context_hint: Phonetic confusion likely due to unclear pronunciation of 'pot of tea'.

style.notes: The speaker tends to use informal language and contractions frequently. There are instances of unclear pronunciation leading to significant transcription errors.
